The '''''Manka Hunter''''' was the name of an [[Incom Corporation/Legends|Incom]] [[Corsair/Legends|corsair]] starship owned by the [[Outlaw tech/Legends|outlaw tech]] [[Shug Ninx]]. He kept it in his [[Spacebarn]] on [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa's]] [[Corellian Sector (Nar Shaddaa)/Legends|Corellian Sector]]. [[Greedo/Legends|Greedo]], a young [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] who was an aspiring [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]], wanted to buy the ship from Ninx for 14,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. Greedo also named the ship, inspired by his uncles, [[Nok (Rodian)|Nok]] and [[Teeku]], who hunted the predatory [[Manka cat/Legends|manka cat]] in the jungles of Greedo's [[U-Tendik|former home planet]].

Greedo put a lot of effort into getting the money together to buy the ship, and spent much of his time fixing it up. At one point, he stole two [[Dekk-6 power coupling]]s from [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] for use on the ''Manka Hunter''. When Chewbacca and Solo discovered the theft, the two [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] decided to teach the youth a lesson by "trading" two burnt-out [[Modog]] couplers from the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] for the Dekk-6's and Greedo's jacket.

Greedo never actually got enough credits or spare parts to get the ''Manka Hunter'' flying. In the aftermath of the [[Battle of Nar Shaddaa (Galactic Civil War)|Imperial attack on Nar Shaddaa]], he left Nar Shaddaa with his bounty hunting mentors, [[Spurch Goa]] and [[Dyyz Nataz]] on their ship, the ''[[Nova Viper]]''. Greedo was killed on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] by Han Solo soon afterward.
